_metadata.description: 12 standard CAR architecture templates with default element selections

standard_2G_hematologic.name: Standard 2nd Generation (Hematologic)
standard_2G_hematologic.description: Classic 2G CAR for B-cell malignancies. Most clinically validated architecture.
standard_2G_hematologic.design_notes: 4-1BB costim: longer persistence, lower CRS. CD28 costim: faster expansion, higher peak. CD8a hinge/TM preferred for proximal epitopes. IgG4 spacer for distal.

tandem_bispecific.name: Tandem Bispecific CAR
tandem_bispecific.description: Two binders in tandem to prevent antigen-loss escape. Standard for CD19/CD22 dual targeting.
tandem_bispecific.design_notes: Binder order matters: membrane-distal binder has better accessibility. Test both orientations. Total construct size may exceed packaging limit for some vectors.

armored_4G.name: Armored/TRUCKs 4th Generation
armored_4G.description: Standard CAR + secreted/anchored cytokine or checkpoint blocker payload.

armor_payload.note: IL-15, IL-18, IL-12, anti-PD-L1 scFv, etc.

armored_4G.design_notes: For solid tumors: combine armor (cytokine) + anti-exhaustion (c-Jun) + homing (CCR2b). Vector size limit ~8kb for LV; consider splitting into two vectors if exceeded.

logic_AND_gate.name: SynNotch AND-Gate
logic_AND_gate.description: Two-signal logic: SynNotch senses antigen 1, triggers expression of CAR targeting antigen 2.
logic_AND_gate.design_notes: Requires two separate lentiviral vectors or large-capacity SB transposon. SynNotch CAR is OFF until priming antigen engagement. Reduces on-target/off-tumor toxicity.

logic_NOT_gate.name: Inhibitory CAR NOT-Gate
logic_NOT_gate.description: Activating CAR + inhibitory iCAR. CAR kills target+ cells but spares target+/blocker+ cells.
logic_NOT_gate.design_notes: iCAR uses PD-1 or CTLA-4 cytoplasmic tail to deliver inhibitory signal. Requires target+ tumor antigen AND blocker antigen on normal tissue.

allo_universal.name: Allogeneic Universal Off-the-Shelf
allo_universal.description: Standard CAR in donor T cells with TCR/MHC knockouts and immune evasion transgenes.
allo_universal.design_notes: TRAC KO is mandatory (prevent GvHD). B2M KO removes MHC-I but triggers NK fratricide → HLA-E transgene rescues. CIITA KO for MHC-II. CD47 prevents macrophage clearance.

car_nk.name: CAR-NK (iPSC or Cord Blood)
car_nk.description: NK cell-optimized CAR with NK-native signaling and persistence cytokine.
car_nk.design_notes: mbIL15 is critical for NK in vivo persistence. 2B4 costim is NK-native. CD16a retains ADCC capacity. iPSC-NK: add BCL11B for T-lineage commitment.

car_treg.name: CAR-Treg (Tolerance Induction)
car_treg.description: CAR in regulatory T cells for autoimmune disease, transplant, or GvHD prevention.

binder.note: Target: donor HLA (transplant) or tissue antigen (autoimmune)

car_treg.design_notes: FoxP3 overexpression is MANDATORY to maintain Treg identity. Helios co-expression stabilizes under inflammation. AVOID pro-inflammatory payloads (IL-12, c-Jun). TNFR2 costim preferred over CD28 for Treg.

car_macrophage.name: CAR-Macrophage (Phagocytic CAR)
car_macrophage.description: CAR in macrophages/monocytes for solid tumor phagocytosis and TME remodeling.
car_macrophage.design_notes: CD68 promoter is MANDATORY for macrophage-restricted expression. CD3z works as phagocytic signal in macrophages. Adenoviral vector preferred (macrophages resist lentiviral transduction). CAR-M remodels TME: M2→M1 polarization.

ipsc_car_t.name: iPSC-Derived CAR-T
ipsc_car_t.description: CAR expressed in iPSC-differentiated T cells with lineage programming factors.

programming_factors.note: BCL11B drives T lineage commitment from iPSC; RUNX3 programs CD8 cytotoxic fate

ipsc_car_t.design_notes: iPSC → hematopoietic progenitor → T cell differentiation requires Notch/DLL4 signaling + BCL11B. All iPSC-CARs are inherently allogeneic → include allo evasion package.

in_vivo_mRNA_car.name: In-Vivo mRNA CAR (LNP Delivery)
in_vivo_mRNA_car.description: CAR delivered as mRNA in T cell-targeting LNP; no ex vivo manufacturing.

targeting_ligand.note: LNP surface ligand for T cell targeting

in_vivo_mRNA_car.design_notes: mRNA expression is TRANSIENT (days, not permanent). Safety advantage: no genomic integration risk. For chronic diseases, may need repeated dosing. No safety switch needed (self-limiting). Alternative integrase: SB100X for stable in vivo expression.

autoimmune_car.name: Autoimmune CAR (Plasma Cell/B Cell Depletion)
autoimmune_car.description: CAR targeting pathogenic B cells or plasma cells for autoimmune disease remission.
autoimmune_car.design_notes: For SLE: BCMA CAR depletes plasma cells producing pathogenic autoantibodies. For pemphigus: CAAR approach (display autoantigen). BCMA preferred over CD19 for long-lived bone marrow plasma cells. Low-dose lymphodepletion is sufficient.
